name: exploiting-sql-injection-vulnerabilities
description: Find and weaponize SQL injection — from a single quote that breaks a query to dumping the whole database with error-based, boolean/time-blind and UNION extraction, then escalating to file read/write and RCE.

name: exploiting-sql-injection-with-sqlmap
description: Drive sqlmap end-to-end on a confirmed injection: fingerprint the DBMS, enumerate databases/tables/columns, dump the data, and pop a shell with --os-shell — automation all the way to the kill.

name: performing-second-order-sql-injection
description: Hunt stored (second-order) SQLi where your payload is saved now and fires later in a different query — the blind spot most scanners and WAFs walk right past.

name: testing-for-xss-vulnerabilities
description: Land a reflected or stored XSS: map the reflection context, craft the breakout sequence, prove a payload actually fires, then weaponize it for cookie theft and session hijacking.

name: testing-for-xss-vulnerabilities-with-burpsuite
description: Use Burp Suite to find and confirm XSS at scale — intercept, fuzz every parameter, escape the output context, and validate a popping payload before you report it.

name: exploiting-template-injection-vulnerabilities
description: Turn Server-Side Template Injection into remote code execution: fingerprint the engine (Jinja2, Twig, Freemarker…) with math probes, then break out of the sandbox to run system commands.

name: performing-directory-traversal-testing
description: Walk out of the web root with ../ and encoding tricks to read arbitrary files (/etc/passwd, app configs, secrets), then chain LFI into code execution via log poisoning or PHP wrappers.

name: testing-for-open-redirect-vulnerabilities
description: Abuse unvalidated redirects to bounce victims from a trusted domain to an attacker page — gold for phishing and for slipping past OAuth/login redirect allowlists.

name: exploiting-server-side-request-forgery
description: Make the server fetch URLs for you: reach internal-only services, scan the private network, and steal cloud credentials from the 169.254.169.254 metadata endpoint.

name: performing-blind-ssrf-exploitation
description: Confirm and exploit blind SSRF with out-of-band callbacks (Burp Collaborator / OAST) when the response leaks nothing — then pivot to internal hosts and cloud metadata.

name: performing-ssrf-vulnerability-exploitation
description: Probe URL parameters for SSRF, escalate to cloud-metadata credential theft (AWS/GCP/Azure), and pivot into the internal network the application implicitly trusts.

name: testing-for-json-web-token-vulnerabilities
description: Break JWT authentication: alg:none, weak-secret cracking, kid/jku injection and algorithm confusion — forge a token and become any user, including admin.

name: exploiting-jwt-algorithm-confusion-attack
description: Pull off RS256-to-HS256 algorithm confusion: sign your own token using the server's public key as the HMAC secret and bypass signature verification completely.

name: performing-jwt-none-algorithm-attack
description: Strip the signature with the 'none' algorithm: tamper the claims, drop the signature, and walk in as admin whenever the server still trusts an unsigned token.

name: testing-cors-misconfiguration
description: Exploit permissive CORS: reflect a malicious Origin with credentials to read authenticated responses cross-site, exfiltrating session data and API secrets from any attacker page.

name: performing-clickjacking-attack-test
description: Build a working clickjacking PoC: frame the target, overlay a decoy, and hijack victim clicks into authenticated actions whenever X-Frame-Options and CSP frame-ancestors are missing.

name: performing-security-headers-audit
description: Read the security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options…) the way an attacker does — spotting the missing defenses that leave the door open to XSS, clickjacking and downgrade attacks.

name: detecting-ai-model-prompt-injection-attacks
description: Craft and understand prompt-injection payloads against LLM apps — override the system prompt, leak hidden instructions, and slip past guardrails (OWASP LLM Top 10 / MITRE ATLAS).

name: performing-web-application-penetration-test
description: Run a full web-app pentest the OWASP WSTG way: recon, map the attack surface, and chain findings into a real compromise — the methodology behind every engagement.

name: performing-subdomain-enumeration-with-subfinder
description: Blow open the attack surface with Subfinder: passively enumerate subdomains from dozens of sources to surface forgotten dev, staging and admin hosts ripe for takeover.

name: scanning-network-with-nmap-advanced
description: Go beyond nmap -sV: NSE scripts, timing/evasion and deep service enumeration to map every open port and pinpoint the soft targets behind them.

name: performing-ssl-tls-security-assessment
description: Assess TLS with sslyze: weak ciphers, expired or mis-issued certificates and protocol downgrades — the crypto gaps that open the door to interception.

name: auditing-aws-s3-bucket-permissions
description: Hunt misconfigured S3 buckets: enumerate names, test anonymous list/read/write, and pull the loot from world-readable buckets — one of the classic instant breaches.

name: conducting-cloud-penetration-testing
description: Attack the cloud (AWS/GCP/Azure): abuse over-permissive IAM, exposed storage and instance metadata to climb from a single foothold to full account takeover.

name: performing-gcp-penetration-testing-with-gcpbucketbrute
description: Brute-force and enumerate Google Cloud Storage buckets with GCPBucketBrute, then test their IAM permissions for the privilege-escalation paths that lead into the project.

name: performing-cve-prioritization-with-kev-catalog
description: Cut through CVE noise: rank what's actually being exploited in the wild using the CISA KEV catalog and EPSS so you hit the vulnerabilities that matter first.

name: performing-dns-enumeration-and-zone-transfer
description: Mine DNS for intel: enumerate records, attempt AXFR zone transfers, and brute-force subdomains to map the target's full footprint before you strike.

name: performing-web-application-firewall-bypass
description: Slip past the WAF: encoding, case and comment tricks plus payload obfuscation to land the SQLi/XSS the filter thought it had blocked.

name: exploiting-api-injection-vulnerabilities
description: Attack API inputs with injection — SQL/NoSQL, OS command injection and SSRF through JSON, GraphQL and headers — hitting the endpoints that skip the web UI's validation.

name: exploiting-nosql-injection-vulnerabilities
description: Exploit NoSQL injection (MongoDB, CouchDB…): operator-injection auth bypass ($ne/$gt) and blind data extraction from APIs that blindly trust JSON input.

name: performing-graphql-introspection-attack
description: Use GraphQL introspection to dump the entire schema, then abuse the hidden queries and mutations the developers never meant to expose.

name: performing-graphql-security-assessment
description: Assess a GraphQL endpoint end-to-end: introspection leaks, injection, batching/DoS and the authorization flaws unique to the query language.
